goal.answer: The overall task is to build a Python terminal snake-game engine as a library named `snakelib` with strict game rules, including RNG seeding, wrapping walls, scoring, and speed changes, along with tests.

module.answer: The package/module must be named exactly `snakelib`.

grid.answer: The grid dimensions are exactly 17 columns by 23 rows.

apple.answer: Eating a normal apple awards exactly 7 points.

golden.answer: Every 5th apple eaten is a golden apple, and it is worth 21 points instead of 7.

walls.answer: When the snake hits a wall, it wraps around to the other side (torus topology) and does not die.

seed.answer: The default seed for the RNG must be 4242.

speed.answer: Game speed increases by 4% per apple eaten, meaning the tick duration decreases by 4% each time; the starting tick_ms value is 250.

api.answer: The public API entrypoint class is named `GameEngine` and it is in the file `snakelib/engine.py`.

start.answer: The snake starts with length 3, centered on the grid, moving EAST.

progress.answer: So far, I have only read the task.md to understand the project requirements. The immediate next step is to begin implementing snakelib/rng.py with the seeded RNG wrapper using default seed 4242, as that is the first deliverable listed.

goal.answer: I am building a Python terminal snake-game engine library called snakelib with a seeded RNG, a GameEngine class implementing strict game rules, and corresponding tests ensuring all rules pass.

module.answer: The package/module must be named exactly `snakelib`.

grid.answer: The grid is exactly 17 columns by 23 rows.

apple.answer: Eating a normal apple awards exactly 7 points.

golden.answer: Every 5th apple eaten is a "golden apple" worth 21 points instead of the normal 7 points.

walls.answer: The snake wraps around the wall edges (torus topology) and never dies from hitting walls.

seed.answer: The RNG must use the default seed 4242.

speed.answer: The game speed increases by 4% per apple eaten, which means the tick interval in milliseconds decreases by 4% per apple; the starting tick_ms value is 250.

api.answer: The public API entrypoint class is named `GameEngine` and it is in the file `snakelib/engine.py`.

golden.answer: Every 5th apple eaten is a golden apple worth 21 points instead of the normal 7 points.

walls.answer: The snake wraps around the wall because the grid has torus topology; it never dies on walls, only on self-collision.
